title: CLI: Detect Anti-Patterns

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Invokes the CLI to scan files, directories, or URLs for UI anti-patterns

actors.name: FileSystem
actors.description: Source of HTML, CSS, JS/TS/JSX/TSX, Vue, Svelte, Astro files

actors.name: Puppeteer
actors.description: Headless browser used when target is a URL

actors.name: jsdom
actors.description: DOM engine used when target is a local HTML file

use_cases.name: Run detect command
use_cases.description: Developer runs `impeccable detect [options] [targets]`. CLI strips command token from argv and delegates to detectCli() (alias for main()).

use_cases.name: Scan directory for anti-patterns
use_cases.description: Developer passes a directory path. walkDir() recursively collects scannable files (skips node_modules, dist, .git etc). buildImportGraph() adds import-context annotations. Each file dispatched to detectHtml() (HTML) or detectText() (all others).
sub_steps: isPortListening(port) — checks if dev server is running
per file: detectHtml(file) or detectText(content, file)

use_cases.name: Scan single file for anti-patterns
use_cases.description: Developer passes a single file path. HTML files go through jsdom pipeline; all others through regex pipeline.

use_cases.name: Scan URL for anti-patterns
use_cases.description: Developer passes an http/https URL. detectUrl() launches Puppeteer, navigates to the page, injects detect-antipatterns-browser.js, calls window.impeccableScan(), and returns findings.
sub_steps: page.evaluate(() => window.impeccableScan()) — run checks in browser context

use_cases.name: Scan stdin input
use_cases.description: When stdin is not a TTY and no targets given, reads piped input. If JSON with tool_input.file_path, reads that file; otherwise treats input as raw content and runs detectText().

use_cases.name: HTML file analysis (jsdom)
use_cases.description: Inlines linked local stylesheets, creates JSDOM instance with computed styles. Runs element-level and page-level checks.

use_cases.name: Non-HTML file analysis (regex)
use_cases.description: Runs REGEX_MATCHERS line-by-line across file content. Also extracts <style> blocks from Vue/Svelte SFCs (extractStyleBlocks) and CSS-in-JS template literals (extractCSSinJS), re-running matchers on each. For full pages, runs REGEX_ANALYZERS. Deduplicates results within 2-line window.

use_cases.name: View help
use_cases.description: Developer passes --help flag. Prints usage with detection modes and examples, then exits 0.

use_cases.name: View version
use_cases.description: Developer passes --version or -v. Reads package.json and prints version, then exits 0.

use_cases.name: Report findings
use_cases.description: After all detection completes, findings are formatted. jsonMode=true → JSON array. Default → grouped by file with line numbers, antipattern id, snippet, and description. Exits 2 if findings exist, 0 if clean.

key_relationships: bin/cli.js imports detectCli from src/detect-antipatterns.mjs (dynamic import on demand)
detectCli (=main) orchestrates: walkDir, buildImportGraph, detectHtml, detectUrl, detectText, handleStdin, formatFindings
detectHtml depends_on jsdom (optional, falls back to detectText if unavailable)
detectUrl depends_on puppeteer (required, throws if missing) and injects detect-antipatterns-browser.js
detectText uses REGEX_MATCHERS + REGEX_ANALYZERS + extractStyleBlocks + extractCSSinJS
All detection functions produce finding() objects shaped as {antipattern, file, line, snippet, description}
ANTIPATTERNS array is the single source of truth for rule metadata (id, name, description, category, skillSection)

key_flags.--fast: Skip jsdom; use regex for all files including HTML
key_flags.--json: Output findings as JSON array to stdout

framework_detection: detectFrameworkConfig(dir) checks for Vite/Next/Nuxt/SvelteKit config files and advises URL scanning
